data.title: Hanstone Gold appoints interim CEO
data.description:
Hanstone Gold (OTCPK:HANCF) (HANS:CA) announced on Friday the appointment of Robert Quinn as interim CEO.

Quinn replaces Raymond Marks.

data.title: North Peak Resources appoints Jim O'Neill as CFO
data.description:
• North Peak Resources (NPR:CA) has appointed Jim O'Neill as Chief Financial Officer and Corporate Secretary of the company, with immediate effect.
  • O'Neill succeeds Andrew Dunlop, who has stepped down from those positions with the Company.
  • O'Neill has over 30 years of experience as a finance executive with multinational businesses operational in Canada, USA, the UK, Turkey, and Kenya, the company said.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gentex declares $0.12 dividend
data.description:
• Gentex (GNTX) declared $0.12/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 2.11%
  • Payable Jan. 21; for shareholders of record Jan. 7; ex-div Jan. 7.
  • See GNTX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Appia Rare Earths & Uranium raises $1.5 million from warrant exercises
data.description:
• Appia Rare Earths & Uranium (APAAF) said on Friday that an aggregate of 10.47M warrants have been exercised since November 1, 2025 for proceeds of $1.57M.
  • This is a result of an increase in the issued and outstanding capital of Appia pursuant to the exercise of warrants, including the acquisition by Kit Tatum of 3M common shares on the exercise of 3 million warrants at $0.15 per warrant, and the expiry of 6.38M warrants held by Kit Tatum, the interest of Kit Tatum in Appia has been reduced to about 8.36% of the issued and outstanding common shares of Appia on a non-diluted basis and a partially diluted basis.
  • Kit Tatum has advised that the 3,000,000 Shares were acquired for investment purposes and that he has no present intention to either increase or decrease his holdings in the company. Notwithstanding the foregoing, he has advised that he may increase or decrease his beneficial ownership, control or direction over common shares of the company through market transactions, private agreements, other treasury issuances or otherwise.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: UK antitrust agency ends inquiry into Synopsys-Ansys merger
data.description:
• The Competition and Markets Authority, the U.K.'s antitrust agency, said on Friday that it had ended its inquiry into the acquisition of Ansys by Synopsys (SNPS). 
  • “The sale of the Divestment Businesses pursuant to the undertakings in lieu of reference completed on 17 October 2025,” the CMA said in a statement. “This brings the merger investigation to a close.”
  • Synopsys divested its optical software tools and photonic software business, whereas ANSYS sold off PowerArtist, a power consumption analysis tool. Both companies sold the assets to Keysight Technologies (KEYS). Keysight completed its acquisitions of both businesses in October. 
  • Synopsys completed its $35B acquisition of Ansys, which was first announced in January 2024, in July.

data.title: Amerigo renews normal course issuer bid
data.description:
• Amerigo Resources (ARG:CA) said on Friday that it will proceed with a new normal course issuer bid.
  • Under the NCIB, Amerigo may purchase for cancellation up to 11.7M common shares of the company, ~10% of Amerigo’s public float as of November 18, 2025. On that date, there were 161.49M issued and outstanding shares of the company, of which 117.27M were forming the public float.
  • Amerigo’s average daily trading volume (“ADTV”) for the six months ending October 31, 2025, was 272,958 Shares. Therefore, the NCIB’s daily purchase limit will be 68,239 shares, which is 25% of the ADTV.
  • The NCIB will commence on December 2, 2025, and may continue until December 1, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Analysts weigh in on the implications of the new UK gaming tax hikes
data.description:
The UK recently announced major changes to its gaming tax regime, with the most significant impacts coming in 2026 and 2027. The Remote Gaming Duty, which covers online casinos and slots, will rise sharply from 21% to 40% starting April 2026. Additionally, a new remote betting tax rate of 25% will apply from April 2027 for profits on online betting, except for UK horseracing bets, which will remain at the lower 15% rate.

Entain (OTCPK:GMVHF) (OTCPK:GMVHY), Flutter Entertainment (FLUT), Intralot S.A. (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY), and Evoke plc (OTCPK:EIHDF) are some of the gaming companies impacted by the new rules.

CBRE Equity Research analyst John DeCree said that while the 40% tax on iGaming was at the high end of the firm's expectation, the worst-case scenario was avoided as horse racing and retail betting were spared. "We suspect mitigation efforts for the higher online gaming tax will be easier relative to retail betting tax increases, which likely would have resulted in significant layoffs and restructuring costs," highlighted DeCree. He also observed that the delayed implementation of the remote sports betting tax to April 2027 is better than expected and gives operators needed time to adapt.

"While the online gaming tax increase is steeper than expected, it appears investors have been bracing for an even worse case scenario, and we view the news largely as a clearing event," summarized DeCree.

CBRE Equity Research kept its Buy ratings on Entain (OTCPK:GMVHF) and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) in place.

Jefferies analyst James Wheatcroft said the firm expects operators to mitigate the new tax structure via reduced marketing/promotion activity, cutting sports sponsorships, and offering less favorable odds to customers. Wheatcroft warned of negative EBITDA consequences for Entain (OTCPK:GMVHF) (OTCPK:GMVHY), Flutter Entertainment (FLUT), and Evoke plc (OTCPK:EIHDF).

data.title: Intel executive home raided in Taiwan: report
data.description:
The Taiwanese home of Intel (INTC) engineer Wei-Jen Lo was raided by authorities in the country amid concerns he may have transferred national security-related technology from his prior employer, The Wall Street Journal reported. 

Taiwanese prosecutors said on Thursday that the house was raided and evidence was seized, including at least one computer, the news outlet added. Assets held by Lo, including real estate and other assets, have also been frozen, according to a court order. 

Lo previously worked at Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and was brought to Intel by CEO Lip-Bu Tan. The 75-year-old Lo retired from Taiwan Semiconductor in July after serving more than 20 years with the company. He was most recently the senior vice president of corporate strategy development for Taiwan Semi. He was instrumental in developing some of TSMC's advanced node technology.

Intel recently hired Lo as vice president of research and development.

Taiwan Semiconductor has allegedly started an internal investigation to determine if Lo took trade secrets regarding advanced node technology. However, Tan steadfastly rejected the allegations in an interview with Bloomberg. “It's rumor and speculation,” Tan said. “There's nothing to it. We respect IP.”

Taiwan Semiconductor has since sued Lo, alleging that he violated the non-compete clause and breached laws, including the Trade Secrets Act, by taking proprietary tech data related to advanced semiconductor processes before leaving the company.

In August, the Trump Administration took an $11.1B, or 10%, stake in Intel. Days later, Intel laid out several new risks associated with the stake, including the belief that other governments could be “unwilling to support the company.”

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Willis Towers Watson in advanced talks to buy Cushon from NatWest - report
data.description:
London-based pensions giant Willis Towers Watson (WTW) is in advanced talks with NatWest (NWG) (OTCPK:RBSPF) for the acquisition of workplace retirement schemes platform Cushon, Sky News reported.

Price under negotiation was unclear on Friday, said the report.

NatWest had bought a controlling 85% stake in Cushon for £144M in 2023, the November 28 report noted.

data.title: French lower house votes to nationalize ArcelorMittal; move likely to fail in Senate
data.description:
The lower house of the French parliament voted late Thursday to nationalize steelmaker ArcelorMittal (MT), although the measure is opposed by the government and likely to be rejected by the Senate.

The legislation, aimed at stopping the company's planned job cuts, was proposed by left-wing parties in the National Assembly, but faces more opposition in the Senate, dominated by centrists and the right, and is widely expected to fail.

ArcelorMittal (MT) announced cost-cutting measures in Europe earlier this year that would cause an estimated 270 job losses in France.

Finance Minister Lescure called the vote a "populist response to a structural problem," and Industry Minister Martin said the law "would weaken employment instead of protecting it," and that the threat to the company comes from a "tsunami" of Asian imports.

China produced 1 billion metric tons of steel in 2024 - more than half of global output - compared with 150 million tons for India and under 100 million tons each for Japan and the U.S., while Europe lags far behind, with Germany producing 37 million tons, Italy 20 million, Spain 12 million and France 11 million.
